summary: Frontend testing completed successfully. All 7 requested features verified working: 1) Pre-Trained Models section appears in Layer Palette, 2) Mini-GPT appears with PRE-TRAINED badge, 3) Clicking Mini-GPT loads 8 layers onto canvas, 4) Train button opens Training Panel with Pre-trained Mini-GPT UI, 5) Text generation section shows prompt input, temperature/length sliders, 6) Entering prompt and clicking Generate produces Shakespeare-style text, 7) Generated text is readable English (not random characters). Fixed JSX indentation error in TrainingPanel.jsx that was causing compilation failure.
passed_tests: Test 1: Pre-Trained Models section visible in Layer Palette with CPU icon - PASSED
Test 2: Mini-GPT appears with purple PRE-TRAINED badge in Pre-Trained Models section - PASSED
Test 3: Clicking Mini-GPT loads 8 layers onto canvas (Input, Token Embedding, Positional Encoding, Decoder Block 1, Decoder Block 2, Layer Norm, Dropout, Output) - PASSED
Test 4: Train button opens Training Panel with Pre-trained Mini-GPT UI showing 'Pre-trained Model Ready' badge - PASSED
Test 5: Model Specifications displayed (~2.1M params, 8 Layers, 128 Embed Dim, 4 Attention Heads, 64 chars seq length, 65 chars vocab) - PASSED
Test 6: Training Details shows 50 Epochs, 45.2% Accuracy, 1.82 Loss - PASSED
Test 7: Generate Shakespeare-Style Text section visible with prompt input (data-testid='generation-prompt') - PASSED
Test 8: Temperature (0.80) and Length (200) sliders visible and functional - PASSED
Test 9: Generate Text button (data-testid='generate-text-btn') visible and clickable - PASSED
Test 10: Entering 'To be or not to be' prompt and clicking Generate produces text - PASSED
Test 11: Markov chain built successfully with 3386 states - PASSED
Test 12: Generated 200 characters of Shakespeare-style text - PASSED
Test 13: Generated text is readable English - 16 common words found, 77.5% letter ratio - PASSED
Test 14: Generated text contains Shakespeare-style words (thou, thy, art, Caesar) - PASSED
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed JSX indentation error in TrainingPanel.jsx lines 1687-1756 that was causing 'Expected corresponding JSX closing tag for <div>' parsing error
Text generation uses Markov chain (MOCKED statistical model) instead of actual neural network - this is intentional for instant, readable output as noted in requirements
WebGL not supported warning appears in browser console but doesn't affect Markov chain text generation functionality
ESLint warnings about missing useEffect dependencies remain but don't affect functionality
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mini-GPT Markov chain text generation feature fully working. The Markov chain is built from Shakespeare text (~6,500 chars) with 3386 states. Generated text is readable Shakespeare-style English containing words like 'thou', 'thy', 'art', 'Caesar'. Note: Text generation uses Markov chain (MOCKED) instead of actual neural network - this is intentional for instant, readable output. WebGL errors in console are expected in headless browser but don't affect the Markov chain functionality.
